planet.name: Edhra
planet.description: An earthlike world of vast oceans, seven continents, and ancient magic. Warmer at the equator, frozen at the poles. Civilizations cluster on the known continent of Valdris; the rest remains shrouded in rumor and legend.

hemispheres.name: Northern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Cold seas, tundra, and the great steppe. Valdris occupies much of this hemisphere's temperate belt.

hemispheres.name: Southern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Tropical coasts giving way to southern ice. Largely unexplored beyond the Iron Coast trade lanes.

hemispheres.name: Western Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Dominated by the Vael Ocean. Valdris sits at its eastern edge; what lies beyond the horizon is the subject of sailor myth.

hemispheres.name: Eastern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: The Ashen Reach and the vast interior of Keth-Morath stretch into this hemisphere. Ancient ruins and dead empires.

continents.name: Valdris
continents.description: The known continent. A rugged landmass of trade cities, blasted wastes, haunted marshes, and warring provinces. Most recorded history unfolds here.

geo_dimensions.scale_note: Roughly the size of Australia. The detail-map viewport (800×600 SVG units, with 25 px padding) maps to this physical area. 1 SVG unit ≈ 3.33 miles ≈ 5.37 km.

continents.name: Keth-Morath
continents.description: A vast eastern continent of crumbling empires and forgotten cities. Nara the Tide-Thief was hunted across its shores. Connected to Valdris by a narrow land bridge or island chain.

continents.name: Thul-Ashara
continents.description: The equatorial continent. Steaming jungles, red deserts, and civilizations older than Valdris. Traders speak of fire-temples and spice lords.

continents.name: Drakmere
continents.description: A volcanic archipelago-continent in the southwestern seas. Dragons are said to roost in its caldera peaks. Smoke columns are visible from passing ships.

continents.name: Sundral
continents.description: A southern continent of sweeping grasslands and deep canyons. Merchant galleons occasionally return with strange spices and stranger tales.

continents.name: Frostholme
continents.description: The northern polar continent. Permanent ice, aurora storms, and the ruins of a pre-human civilization buried under glaciers.

continents.name: Ashenveil
continents.description: The southern polar continent. Eternally shrouded in grey mist. Ships that sail too far south do not return. Its very existence is debated by scholars.

macro_regions.name: The Steppe Marches
macro_regions.description: Vast northern grasslands swept by bitter winds. Nomadic warbands, living fortresses, and contested border keeps. The Bitter Sea gnaws at its northern coast.

macro_regions.name: The Shattered West
macro_regions.description: War-torn highlands and dark forests. Once a unified kingdom, now fragmented into squabbling provinces, haunted ruins, and witch-held holdfasts.

macro_regions.name: The Sunken Marches
macro_regions.description: Waterlogged lowlands where fog never fully lifts. Ghost-kings, drowned villages, and deep enchanted forests where beast-spirits guard the old ways.
macro_regions.terrain: marsh, bog, flooded forest, river delta

macro_regions.name: The Hearthlands
macro_regions.description: The civilized heartland of Valdris. River valleys, rolling farmland, and the great trade city of Pelimor. Crowns and councils vie for power; merchant guilds pull the strings.

macro_regions.name: The Ashen Reach
macro_regions.description: A vast eastern expanse of blasted wastes, cursed deserts, and ancient tombs. The Ironspine Mountains wall it off from the Hearthlands. Caravan routes are its only arteries.

macro_regions.name: The Iron Coast
macro_regions.description: The southern seaboard of Valdris. Port cities, fishing villages, and the shimmering Sapphire Sea. Merfolk nations lurk beneath the waves; pirates raid the trade lanes.

natural_features.name: The Vael Ocean
natural_features.description: The great western ocean separating Valdris from whatever lies beyond. Storms rage in its heart; only the boldest captains venture past the sight of land.

natural_features.name: The Sapphire Sea
natural_features.description: The warm southern sea. Merfolk nations dwell in its depths. Tide-magic pulses through its currents. The Iron Coast draws its wealth from these waters.

natural_features.name: The Bitter Sea
natural_features.description: Cold northern waters between Valdris and Frostholme. Ice floes drift south in winter. Few harbors survive its fury.

natural_features.name: The Ironspine Mountains
natural_features.description: A jagged north-south spine dividing the Hearthlands from the Ashen Reach. Dwarven holds tunnel through its roots. Passes are few and treacherous.

natural_features.name: The Pelimor River
natural_features.description: The great trade river of the Hearthlands. Flows south from the foothills of the Ironspine through Pelimor and into the Sapphire Sea.

natural_features.name: The Ashwater
natural_features.description: A seasonal river rising on the eastern slopes of the Ironspine Mountains. It flows east and southeast through the Ashen Reach, often reduced to a dry bed in summer. When it flows, it carries ashen silt from the Scorched Flats to the eastern coast.

natural_features.name: The Greymist River
natural_features.description: A slow, fog-shrouded river that drains the Sunken Marches, flowing south and southwest to meet the coast where the Vael Ocean and Sapphire Sea merge. Its waters are said to carry whispers of the drowned dead.

natural_features.name: The Northmere
natural_features.description: A broad, cold river that rises in the northern Ironspine and flows northwest across the Steppe Marches to empty into the Bitter Sea. Frozen solid for three months each winter; the rest of the year it carries furs and grain toward the coast.

natural_features.name: The Ironbrook
natural_features.description: A fast mountain stream that tumbles from the Ironspine's western foothills to join the Pelimor River. Dwarven runoff taints its waters rust-red in spring.

natural_features.name: The Wychflow
natural_features.description: The main river of the Shattered West. It rises where the Ironspine foothills meet the western highlands, then winds west and southwest through dark forests and ruined provinces to empty into the Vael Ocean. Witch-lights dance on its surface at night.

natural_features.name: The Coldvein
natural_features.description: A small, icy tributary of the Wychflow, flowing southeast from the highland moors. Its water is unnaturally cold — locals say a frost-spirit sleeps in its source.

natural_features.name: The Thornwater
natural_features.description: A sluggish, briar-choked river that rises in the western Hearthlands hills, flowing south and southeast to join the Pelimor River. Its banks are tangled with witch-thorn.

routes.name: Pelimor–Ashen Caravan Route
routes.travel_note: Three to four weeks by laden caravan; ten days hard ride.
routes.description: The great caravan road connecting Pelimor's markets to the Ashen Wastes. Bandits, toll-lords, and spice merchants ply this route.

routes.name: The North Road
routes.travel_note: Two weeks by horse, longer in winter when snow closes the passes.
routes.description: The main highway running north from Pelimor through the Hearthlands and into the Steppe Marches. Pieter Vane fled three provinces along it.

routes.name: The Iron Coast Sea Lane
routes.travel_note: Four to six days by merchant galley; faster with tide-magic.
routes.description: Coastal and deep-sea shipping lanes along the Iron Coast. Connects port cities to the merfolk realm.
